title: Simulating Reads for Detection of Transportable Element Insertions
author: Qichao Yu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.imrcc56

steps.title: Generate N random numbers from 1...
steps.instruction: Generate N random numbers from 1 to M (M is the total number of TEs) nonredundantly. In our study, N=761, M=2852.

steps.title: Generate X random numbers from 1 to 22 nonredundantly
steps.instruction: Generate X random numbers from 1 to 22 nonredundantly. X is the number of autosomes picked up for TE insertion.

steps.title: According to the lengths of the...
steps.instruction: According to the lengths of the X autosomes, assign the N TEs to these X autosomes proportionably.

steps.title: Generate corresponding number of random numbers...
steps.instruction: Generate corresponding number of random numbers for these X autosomes from 1 to the length of the chromosome. These numbers are the positions that TEs will be inserted.

steps.title: Insert the TE sequence to a...
steps.instruction: Insert the TE sequence to a specific position of a specific autosome in order. Then it produces a new FASTA file (hg19_TE.fasta).

steps.title: Run PIRS using the resulting FASTA file
steps.instruction: Run PIRS using the resulting FASTA file. Our command line is "pirs -i hg19_TE.fasta -o output.fastq.prefix -x 60".
